title: gustavia terminaliflora
intro: Gustavia terminaliflora, also known as the 'Brazilian cherry' or 'Guayabilla', is a tropical plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gustavia terminaliflora include the potential treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties and the ability to reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Gustavia terminaliflora can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
